mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires printer enrollment methods, API helpers for enrollment, or page objects for enrollment flow. Batch 5 contains YAML test case definitions for Shipment Tracking and Update Plan pages (not enrollment flows) and the main aura.py framework entry point (which provides CLI and configuration but no enrollment-specific methods). No enrollment page objects, API helpers, or enrollment methods found in this batch. Enrollment components likely exist in batches containing enrollment flow implementations or API helper modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires API helpers to retrieve subscription details and verify no free months. Batch 5 contains no API helper classes or subscription management methods. The aura.py file provides framework infrastructure but no subscription-specific API calls. Subscription API helpers likely exist in batches containing API utility modules or subscription management helpers.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires API helpers to get subscription status and verify it is 'subscribed'. Batch 5 contains no subscription status checking methods or API helpers. The YAML test files reference external login/logout flows but do not contain subscription status verification. Subscription status verification methods likely exist in batches containing API helper modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires page objects with pause plan button/link locators and click methods. Batch 5 YAML files test Update Plan page which mentions 'Cancel Instant Ink' hyperlink but not 'Pause Instant Ink'. The YAML files use AI-based visual validation rather than element locators. No page objects, locators for pause plan buttons, or click methods found in this batch. Dashboard page objects with pause plan functionality likely exist in batches containing page object implementations.

test_case_mappings.no_match_reason: Test case C44873414 requires automation of the Print and Payment History page for HP Instant Ink subscription management, specifically testing the Billing Cycle Period card behavior during plan pause scenarios. The test requires: (1) Page objects for Print and Payment History page and Dashboard page with navigation methods, (2) Locators for billing cycle card, plan pause information, complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages progress bar, info icons, tooltips, value text displays, and total pages printed text, (3) Action methods for navigation, scrolling, hovering (desktop), clicking (mobile), and page refresh, (4) Verification methods for element visibility, text content, progress bar colors (black/yellow), progress bar fill percentage, and tooltip content, (5) API helpers for printer enrollment, subscription status checking, plan pause, time shift (32 days), billing charge trigger, and page printing simulation, (6) Test data constants for URLs, page limits (10 complimentary, 10 additional block), expected text values, and tooltip messages. Batch 5 contains: (1) Four YAML test case definition files (TC31266486, TC31266491, TC31266518, TC31409312) that test Shipment Tracking and Update Plan pages using AI-powered visual validation approach - these are test definitions, not reusable code components, and they test different pages (Shipment Tracking and Update Plan, not Print and Payment History), (2) The aura.py main framework entry point file which provides command-line interface, argument parsing, configuration building, flow orchestration, platform configurations, and utility functions for requirements parsing and flow scaffolding - this is framework infrastructure, not test-specific components. None of these files contain page objects, locators, action methods, verification methods, API helpers, or test data constants needed for the Print and Payment History page test. The YAML files use AI-based visual validation with screenshot comparison and validation prompts rather than traditional element-based automation with locators and methods. The aura.py file provides framework-level utilities but no page-specific or test-specific components. The required components for this test case (Print and Payment History page object, billing cycle card locators, progress bar verification methods, API helpers for subscription management and page printing, etc.) likely exist in other batches containing page object implementations, API helper modules, or utility classes for HP Instant Ink functionality.
